May 13 at 8:55 AM

can you create a detailed prompt to copy the layout and structure except for colors(should use my project theme)

Here's a detailed prompt you can copy and reuse to recreate this exact layout/structure in another project (it will adopt that project's theme colors automatically since it only uses semantic tokens):

Prompt:

Create a page at the route /voucher that displays 3 "Access Voucher" pricing cards in a modern, minimalist style. Use only the project's existing semantic design tokens (e.g. bg-background, text-foreground, bg-card, text-card-foreground, text-muted-foreground, border-border, bg-primary, text-primary-foreground, bg-accent) — do NOT hardcode any colors. Use lucide-react icons.

Page structure:

  1. Full-height <main> with min-h-screen bg-background, horizontal padding px-4, vertical padding py-16.
  2. Inner container mx-auto max-w-5xl.
  3. Centered <header> with bottom margin mb-12:
    • <h1>: "Access Vouchers" — text-3xl font-semibold tracking-tight text-foreground sm:text-4xl.
    • <p> subtitle: "Pick a plan. Pay once. Connect instantly." — mt-3 text-sm text-muted-foreground.
  4. <section aria-label="Voucher plans"> as a responsive grid: grid gap-4 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3.

Card data (3 plans, middle one featured):

  • 1 Hour — $2, "per voucher" — features: "Full speed access", "1 device", "Instant activation".
  • 6 Hours — $8, "per voucher" — features: "Full speed access", "1 device", "Pause anytime", "Best for a session". (featured: true)
  • 24 Hours — $15, "per voucher" — features: "Full speed access", "2 devices", "Pause anytime", "Priority support".

Each card (<article>):

  • Base classes: rounded-md border bg-card p-6 transition-colors.
  • If featured: add border-foreground/80. Otherwise: border-border hover:border-foreground/40.
  • Header row: flex items-center gap-2 text-muted-foreground containing a Clock icon (h-4 w-4) and an <h2> "{duration} Access" — text-sm font-medium.
  • Price row (mt-4 flex items-baseline gap-1.5): price in text-4xl font-semibold tracking-tight text-card-foreground, unit in text-xs text-muted-foreground.
  • Divider: my-6 h-px bg-border.
  • Features list (<ul class="space-y-2.5">): each <li> is flex items-start gap-2 text-sm text-card-foreground, with a Check icon mt-0.5 h-4 w-4 shrink-0 text-muted-foreground followed by the feature text.
  • CTA button ("Get voucher"): mt-6 w-full rounded-md px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium transition-colors.
    • Featured: bg-primary text-primary-foreground hover:bg-primary/90.
    • Non-featured: border border-border bg-background text-foreground hover:bg-accent.

SEO: set route head with title "Vouchers | Access Plans" and meta description "Choose an access voucher: 1 hour, 6 hours, or 24 hours."

Style notes: subtle/minimal rounded corners (rounded-md only), thin 1px borders, no shadows, no gradients, generous whitespace, monochrome — visual hierarchy comes purely from typography weight/size and the project's theme tokens.

May 14 at 3:02 PM

can you create a prompt for that

Here's a detailed prompt you can copy and reuse to recreate this login page in another project (it will adopt that project's theme automatically since it only uses semantic tokens):

Prompt:

Create a modern minimalist login page at the route /login for an inventory management system. Use only the project's existing semantic design tokens (bg-background, text-foreground, bg-muted, border-border, text-muted-foreground, etc.) — never hardcode colors. Use shadcn Button, Input, Label, and Select components, and lucide-react icons.

Layout — split screen (2 columns on lg, single column on mobile):

  • Left panel (hidden below lg): border-r border-border bg-muted/30 p-10, flex column with justify-between.

    • Top: small brand row — a 32px square rounded-md border bg-background containing a Boxes icon, next to the text "Inventory OS" (text-sm font-medium tracking-tight).
    • Middle: a centered illustration (generate or use a minimalist line-art image of organized shelves with tools and boxes). Constrain with max-h-[60vh] w-auto object-contain opacity-90. Include loading="lazy", width, height, and meaningful alt text.
    • Bottom: a tagline block (max-w-sm) — heading "Track every tool. Trust every check-out." (text-lg font-medium tracking-tight) and a muted subline.

  • Right panel: centered form, max-w-sm container, padded px-6 py-12 sm:px-10.

    • Mobile-only brand row at the top (same as left panel's top, shown only below lg).
    • Heading: "Welcome back" (text-2xl font-semibold tracking-tight) + muted subheading "Sign in to continue to your dashboard."
    • Role switch (segmented control, not tabs component): a 2-column grid inside rounded-md border border-border bg-muted/40 p-1, with two <button>s for "admin" and "toolkeeper". The active one gets bg-background text-foreground shadow-sm; inactive gets text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground. Use role="tablist" / role="tab" / aria-selected for a11y. Track selection with useState<"admin" | "toolkeeper">.
    • Form below switches fields based on role.

Field component pattern:

A reusable Field wrapper that renders a small uppercase-ish muted Label (text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground) above the input, with a leading icon absolutely positioned inside the input (absolute left-3 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 h-4 w-4 text-muted-foreground). Use a wrapper div with [&_input]:pl-9 [&_button]:pl-9 so both Inputs and Select triggers get left padding for the icon.

Admin fields (when role === "admin"):

  • Username — User icon, placeholder "admin", autoComplete="username".
  • Password — Lock icon, type="password", placeholder "••••••••", autoComplete="current-password".

Toolkeeper fields (when role === "toolkeeper"):

  • Name — User icon, autoComplete="name".
  • Student ID — Hash icon, inputMode="numeric", placeholder like "2024-00123".
  • A 2-column grid-cols-2 gap-3 row containing:
    • Year level — GraduationCap icon, shadcn Select with options "1st Year" through "4th Year".
    • Section — Layers icon, plain Input.

Submit: full-width primary Button labeled "Sign in". Prevent default on submit (no backend yet).

Footer: small muted centered text under the form: "By signing in, you agree to follow the lab inventory policy." (text-xs text-muted-foreground).

SEO: route head() with title "Sign In | Inventory Management" and a matching description.

Style notes: subtle rounded corners only (rounded-md, rounded-sm), thin 1px borders, no gradients, no heavy shadows (only shadow-sm on the active segment), generous whitespace, monochrome — visual hierarchy comes from typography weight/size and the project's theme tokens.
